title: Three courts draw AI lines as Tesla bets on robots - TCR 05/03/26
description:
A Texas small claims court awarded $10,672 against Tesla for undelivered Full Self-Driving on a $73 filing fee, the Hangzhou Intermediate People's Court upheld that workers cannot be dismissed for AI adoption, and the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences ruled Oscar performances must be demonstrably human with consent.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-3-2026/

0:00 Hangzhou ruling on AI labor substitution
2:40 The Hangzhou Pattern
6:34 Tesla $573 million related-party disclosure and Optimus factory
9:31 Spirit Airlines shutdown and Iran fuel shock
12:18 Ohio floating solar and distributed energy ground signals
14:50 Close

This episode covers the Travis County FSD default judgment and replicable template, the Hangzhou intermediate court ruling on AI labor substitution, the Academy's human-consent rule for Oscar eligibility, Tesla's $573 million in related-party SEC disclosures with xAI and SpaceX, the Fremont Optimus factory conversion targeting one million humanoid robots per year, Spirit Airlines' shutdown and 17,000 lost jobs, and the Iran-driven fuel shock accelerating EV adoption to 26% market share in New Zealand.

Three rulings landed yesterday at a tier of the institutional stack the capital is not pricing against. A $73 small claims filing in Travis County converted every undelivered FSD buyer into a potential plaintiff with a working template. The Hangzhou Intermediate People's Court published reasoning that other Chinese intermediate courts will cite when the next worker is offered a 40% pay cut to be replaced by an LLM. The Academy drew a human-consent line around performance and authorship as an independent film advances production using an AI-generated Val Kilmer. None of the rulings resolves the underlying capability question. Each holds that the substitution AI now enables cannot occur on existing terms inside that jurisdiction. Meanwhile Tesla disclosed $573 million in related-party flows from xAI and SpaceX and announced its Fremont Model S and Model X lines will be converted to a million-unit-per-year Optimus humanoid robot factory, the same week Meta acquired Assured Robot Intelligence. Spirit Airlines collapsed before dawn Saturday under the Iran-conflict fuel shock that simultaneously pushed New Zealand plugin sales up 278% year-over-year with no government incentives.
